Maternal, Child, Adolescent Program (MCAH)
Maternal, Child, Adolescent Program (MCAH)
We develop and implement programs that protect and improve maternal, infant, child, and adolescent health, and conduct comprehensive needs assessments every five years. We are essential partners in accomplishing the statewide MCAH Program mission and goals. We work with other local programs to ensure access to health care, early prenatal care, well well-child checks, assist with resources, provide Developmental screenings and referrals if needed, provide home visits in conjunction with the Family First Program including oral health screening and education, assist with breastfeeding issues, provide preconception health in our clinics and many more education and referrals as needed.
The SIDS Program is a part of the MCAH Program. Find
more information on the SIDS Program
.
For more information, please feel free to contact the
Public Health Agency Clinic
: (530) 283-6330
